purpose: Broader Reactome gene-set layer for the NB5 four-way node-level comparison, built as an explicit manually curated UNION of independently verified Reactome pathway accessions related to human pigmentation biology -- NOT a single pathway's descendant tree. IMPORTANT CORRECTION: an earlier attempt at this task assumed R-HSA-5619507 was named 'Pigmentation' and was the parent of Melanin biosynthesis (R-HSA-5662702), MC1R signalling, and melanosome trafficking as siblings. That assumption is FALSE: R-HSA-5619507 resolves to 'Activation of HOX genes during differentiation', an unrelated developmental pathway. Reactome has no single named 'Pigmentation' pathway, and Melanin biosynthesis (under Metabolism > Metabolism of amino acids and derivatives) and the MITF-regulation pathways (under Developmental Biology) sit in disjoint hierarchy branches with no shared low-level parent. This snapshot instead documents a deliberate, itemized union of four independently confirmed, verified-live accessions.

components.role: precision terminal-enzyme reference (also frozen standalone in reactome_R-HSA-5662702_participants.json)

MITF's broad transcriptional program in melanocytes -- the largest verified real Reactome pathway substantially overlapping pigmentation biology (includes pigmentation-gene targets TYR/TYRP1/DCT/MLANA/RAB27A/MYO5A/GPR143/PMEL/SOX10/PAX3/MC1R/KIT/EDNRB/POMC, plus many MITF targets in cell-cycle, apoptosis, EMT, and lysosome biogenesis whose link to pigmentation specifically is indirect -- included here in full rather than hand-filtered, and flagged in the notebook discussion as an honest breadth-vs-precision tradeoff).

MC1R and its paralogs MC2-5R (MC1R is the canonical pigmentation-relevant melanocortin receptor; MC2-5R are included as the DefinedSet's full membership rather than hand-picking MC1R alone, and are flagged as not pigmentation-specific in the notebook).
disease pathway for the one core pigmentation gene (SLC24A5) not already captured by the other three components' gene sets.
